refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and ensuring rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability KPI is decomposed to focus on minimizing downtime and ensuring rapid fault recovery, which are key contributors to availability. This refinement clarifies measurable sub-functions to guide design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure system uptime and downtime over the evaluation period; verify fault detection and recovery times meet target thresholds.
sub_frs: Enable rapid fault detection and recovery

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms
Real-time health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Robust fault detection and automated recovery procedures
selected_dp.selected_dp: Real-time health monitoring and predictive maintenance combined with robust fault detection and automated recovery procedures

potential_sources: Interaction between fault detection and recovery logic
Dependency on telemetry data for health monitoring
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically groups health monitoring and fault recovery to minimize coupling with other functions; interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Risk of delayed fault detection impacting availability
Risk of incomplete recovery procedures causing extended downtime
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit separation of predictive maintenance and fault recovery if coupling increases in detailed design.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Implements health monitoring, fault detection, and recovery logic to ensure system availability and safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time system status and sensor data for health monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts on detected faults impacting safety and availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other modules for coordination of recovery actions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on detailed health monitoring and recovery.
Physical component focus; less suitable for centralized monitoring logic.
Focuses on operator control, not system health or availability.
Best containment for health monitoring and fault recovery with minimal diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing availability-related health monitoring and recovery in Safety Services minimizes diffusion and aligns with safety criticality.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Safety Services module responsibilities
Dependency on network link reliability for recovery coordination
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity; consider splitting recovery logic if Safety Services becomes too complex.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FRs are clearly addressed by selected DPs with logical mapping and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk due to network link dependency for recovery coordination.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services module provides strong containment for availability-related functions.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and fault logs is feasible within Safety Services module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of availability functions to Safety Services with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Review Safety Services module complexity in subsequent design iterations.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and allocation meets criteria for clarity, containment, and verification feasibility with acceptable risk levels.
next_level_sub_frs: Enable rapid fault detection and recovery

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
Network link supports required communication for recovery.
open_risks: Potential complexity overload in Safety Services module.
Impact of network link failures on recovery coordination.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data quality and latency metrics.
Module complexity metrics during detailed design.
